2606.11120.title: Monte Carlo Pass Search: Using Trajectory Generation for 3D Counterfactual Pass Evaluation in Football
2606.11120.paper_summary: We recast pass evaluation in football (soccer) as a Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS)-like evaluation problem whose components mostly exist in the literature under different names: a value model (possession value), a world model (multi-agent trajectories with ball interactions), and a policy over counterfactual actions (sampling pass variants with noise). Building on the first public high-fidelity tracking dataset with 3D ball trajectories from the Bundesliga, we introduce Monte Carlo Pass Search (MCPS), which infers kick parameters for each observed pass, samples execution variants and option variants, rolls each candidate forward with a ball-conditioned world model until the next ball interaction, and scores outcomes with a learned value model to obtain a distribution over gained value. This distribution enables distribution-aware attribution with two complementary execution-surplus scores used for analysis and ranking: mean-based and percentile-based scores. To make the world model sample-efficient under limited public data, we adapt a discrete-token, autoregressive trajectory generator from autonomous driving (SMART) and show it yields strong best-of-20 forecasting accuracy compared to baselines, while supporting fully hypothetical rollouts for downstream evaluation. We have released model checkpoints and code.
2606.11120.paper_summary_zh: 我們將足球（足球）的傳球評估重新構建為類似於蒙地卡羅樹搜索（MCTS）的評估問題，其組成部分在文獻中通常以不同的名稱存在：價值模型（控球價值）、世界模型（多智能體的球互動軌跡）以及針對反事實行動的策略（帶噪聲的傳球變體取樣）。基於來自德甲的首個公開高保真追蹤數據集，該數據集包含3D球軌跡，我們引入了蒙地卡羅傳球搜索（MCPS），該方法推斷每個觀察到的傳球的踢球參數，取樣執行變體和選項變體，並使用基於球的世界模型將每個候選者向前推進直到下一次球的互動，並使用學習到的價值模型對結果進行評分，以獲得獲得價值的分佈。這一分佈使得可以進行分佈感知的歸因，並使用兩個互補的執行盈餘分數進行分析和排名：基於均值的分數和基於百分位的分數。為了使世界模型在有限的公共數據下高效取樣，我們從自動駕駛（SMART）中調整了一個離散標記、自回歸的軌跡生成器，並顯示其在與基準相比時，能夠提供強大的最佳20預測準確性，同時支持完全假設的展開以進行下游評估。我們已經發布了模型檢查點和代碼。
2606.11120.author: Andrew Kang et.al.
